name: Nimbu Pani
tagline: Indian lemonade with rock salt and mint — hydration plus vitamin C
highlight: Black salt + roasted cumin = rehydration + digestion in one glass

hero.alt: Tall glass of lemon water with mint leaves and ice cubes
hero.caption: nimbu pani — minted, salted, ice-cold

healthFacts: One large lemon = 30mg vitamin C — about 30% of daily target.
Black salt adds sulfur compounds and trace minerals; less sodium than table salt.
Cumin seed water aids digestion and supports gut motility.
Sugar-free version (just salt + lemon) is ideal for blood sugar control.

steps.title: Squeeze the lemons
steps.detail: Roll the lemons firmly on the counter to break the membranes — gives more juice. Cut and squeeze into a jug.
steps.tip: Strain the seeds out — but keep some pulp for vitamin C and fiber.

steps.title: Mix and balance
steps.detail: Add cold water, black salt, roasted cumin, and sugar/jaggery if using. Stir well. Taste and adjust — should be tangy with a savoury edge.
steps.tip: Skip sugar entirely if making for daily/diabetic-friendly drinking.

steps.title: Mint and ice
steps.detail: Crush mint leaves between your palms to release oils. Drop into the jug along with ice. Pour and serve immediately.
steps.tip: Pre-make and refrigerate without ice — top up with ice when serving.
